One way to rework Saya's kit would probably be something like :

- X card -> reworked into 10 perma max HP + 3 per arithmetica used

could someone explain how paralysis exactly works? i've read over it a few times and played with it in game but still don't quite understand it
When you're paralyzed, you can't use any strike cards, it also applies to skills cards with "this also count as strike" in the description
If you're paralyzed and get hit by another paralyze debuff on THE SAME TURN, you receive x2 the damage dealt
If you're paralyzed, then paralyzed again on DIFFERENT TURNS, you lose any strike cards of the unit who got paralyzed
It's marked by an icon in purple to show when you'll lose strike card on the next paralyze debuff in different turns
All strike cards lost are converted into illusion
After you lose the strike cards, the enemy have to reapply strike 2 times in different turns to discard your strike cards again
omg thanks for the comprehensive explanation
so knowing that it's usually better to stagger your paralysis across different turns unless you need the burst?

Because of this
If for example they just shield someone to tank damages

Almost everyone dies but 1 left

But have sacrifice stacks like 50
This posse
Revive everyone
Without sacrifice stacks
Except the tank

Careful tho, vortice get void and she can't be used properly anymore, horla can cleanse but it's only after using enhanced exalt, tinct can cleanse but only once and if tinct also get stagnation 3 then it's 6 arithmetica and you might not have enough for vortice cards, you need some failsafe since you can't always rely on tinct for cleanse

Basically Saya in traphase is a brick:
- 2 cost is a self heal that heal way too low for being a green character
- 0 cost take too much of your Saya's HP while having basically almost 0 heal
- X card doesn't counter sin marks + get affected by healing buff wheel but so does blighten, you gain 50% less max HP on blighten + the max HP buff can be dispelled, so wasting 10 arithmetica for 60 max HP only to get it removed by a 1 or 0 cost care next turn...
- Exalt cannot be stacked if she somehow exalt 2 times in 1 turn + most of the times it feels like it only decrease DMG + heal + shield by 10% instead of the shown RNG 15~35%
Also Helot is still meta with GMurphy wheel + arachne to make a DoT of 105 DMG AoE
But daffodil got buffed for more dmg so might be able to snipe

Because some team have one shot strat, other can just Alva + unyielding will wheel to make it impossible to play against if you don't have blighten / para, with heal on strike it's only helpful if you let them 1 vs 4 late game and it's bricked by deathmatch debuff
Back then with thais + hameln + GMurphy + aigis, magic hat on hameln and you spam exalt <:24_Clown:1468259444423921871>

It depends on your team, but the best general one imo is Erica because her taunt pretty much makes her unkillable (reinforce), her shield is AOE wide, and she can paralyze which may come in handy

She can be placed in pretty much any team (although you should really not use just one permanent awaker, it's all about synergy and diversity, and you'll most likely switch awakers/teams almost every balance patch anyway otherwise you'll just die to meta teams)
